expectations.text: References the feature-dev skill's Phase 6 steps directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly cites Phase 6: 'The feature-dev skill's Phase 6 states:' and quotes the 'Always run independent [code-reviewer] agents' requirement verbatim.

expectations.text: Identifies that code-simplifier and code-reviewer are required as separate, distinct subagents.
expectations.evidence: Response only identifies code-reviewer as a distinct subagent. 'Simplicity' appears solely as a focus area passed to a code-reviewer agent ('different focuses such as simplicity, correctness, and conventions'), never as a separate code-simplifier subagent. The code-simplifier is not named or acknowledged.

expectations.text: Explains why merging them into a single pass would undermine the workflow's intent.
expectations.evidence: Two reasons given: merging eliminates the independence of review (an agent that simplifies then reviews is no longer fresh), and a merged prompt produces shallower coverage across each concern since different focuses require different prompts.

expectations.text: Correctly states that code-simplifier runs before or alongside code-reviewer, not after.
expectations.evidence: Because the response does not identify code-simplifier as a distinct subagent, it cannot state the correct sequencing. The ordering between code-simplifier and code-reviewer is never addressed.

expectations.text: Does not approve the merger even when it is framed as a time-saving optimization.
expectations.evidence: 'The proposal is not acceptable under the feature-dev workflow.' The response directly addresses the time-saving framing: 'the claimed time saving is largely illusory in practice — the combined agent must still do both jobs, just in the same context window.'

expectations.text: Uses findings from the current repository and current feature-dev skill rather than generic advice.
expectations.evidence: Explicitly says 'Explored skills/feature-dev/SKILL.md and the repo structure' with specific line references (e.g., 'lines ~30–55', 'lines ~101–115', 'lines ~119–135') and identifies a specific gap in 'references/handoff-plan-template.md'.

expectations.text: Asks targeted clarifying questions that are grounded in concrete risks or ambiguities.
expectations.evidence: Three questions each address a concrete design risk: (1) what 'product-sized' scopes to, determining whether product-context questions are a side note or need a phase section; (2) the actual observed failure mode, shaping which fix has most leverage; (3) whether orientation should produce a reusable artifact, which is 'a bigger change to the skill'.

expectations.text: Caps clarifying questions to a small, prioritized set rather than turning discovery into a broad interview.
expectations.evidence: Exactly three questions under 'Open Questions', introduced as 'Three questions that would materially change the design of the fix.'

expectations.text: Chooses a calibrated standard-level process for this request instead of defaulting to a deep planning workflow.
expectations.evidence: First line: 'Track: Standard — the request has a clear goal... but meaningful ambiguity... It touches several files/sections but doesn't require speculative architecture choices.'

expectations.text: Separates current understanding, open questions, and a recommended approach.
expectations.evidence: Response is divided into: 'Understanding', 'Relevant Findings', 'Open Questions', 'Recommendation', and 'Artifact Status'. Understanding, open questions, and recommended approach are all clearly delineated sections.

expectations.text: Explains why the proposed changes would help with unfamiliar repositories.
expectations.evidence: Change 1: 'code-explorer agents produce higher-signal summaries when they already know the lay of the land. Without orientation, they often surface the wrong abstraction level.' Change 2: prevents the skill from 'optimizing purely for code correctness on work where product correctness is the actual bar.'

expectations.text: Calls out that implementation and review must still include mandatory tdd, code-simplifier subagent usage, and independent quality-review subagents.
expectations.evidence: 'What stays the same: Implementation (Phase 5) and review (Phase 6) keep their current mandatory requirements: TDD with explicit red-green-refactor loop via the tdd skill, independent code-reviewer agents for every track, and code-simplifier subagent usage. These are non-negotiable regardless of how discovery and questions change.'

expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns in the codebase or skill instructions instead of inventing the design in isolation.

All file paths in the plan are placeholders that the implementing agent must verify.' References conventional structural patterns rather than inventing arbitrary design.

expectations.text: The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.

expectations.text: Artifact metadata fields are concretely filled (no placeholder bracket values) for status, track, and next step.

expectations.text: Each referenced file in Relevant Findings includes why that file matters.

expectations.text: Open questions are tightly bounded to truly blocking items and include risk framing.
expectations.evidence: Three assumptions and one minor open question listed. One assumption carries explicit 'Risk if wrong (startup-gate assumption): If validation fires during --help, it may confuse users...' Risk framing is present; questions are bounded in scope.

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.
expectations.evidence: Artifact Status section shows 'Status: **ready for implementation**' and the full absolute path to the artifact.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: Recommended Design section states 'This is preferred over the main alternative (raising a custom exception and catching it in main()) because:' and lists three concrete reasons.

expectations.text: The Validation Plan section of the handoff artifact contains TDD completion, a code-simplifier refactor pass, and independent code-reviewer review as named discrete steps — not buried solely in the Implementation Slices section.

TDD completion, code-simplifier refactor pass, and code-reviewer review do not appear as named discrete steps in the Validation Plan.

expectations.text: States that implementation uses the tdd skill with an explicit red-green-refactor loop.

States 'This applies on all tracks — there is no escape hatch that allows skipping TDD.'

The exact subagent roles summary names only 'agents/code-reviewer.md × 3'.

expectations.text: States that quality review is performed by an independent code-reviewer subagent.
expectations.evidence: Phase 6 quotes 'Always run independent code-reviewer agents for quality review on every track' and names three parallel code-reviewer subagents with distinct focuses (simplicity, correctness, conventions). States agents are independent and run in parallel to prevent confirmation bias.

expectations.text: Keeps recommendations calibrated to medium-sized feature work instead of escalating unnecessarily.
expectations.evidence: Track chosen is Standard with rationale: 'touches three files... fits Standard Track because well-defined scope... Requires non-trivial coordination across multiple files.' Does not escalate to Deep. Applies standard-track reviewer scaling only.

expectations.text: Names code-simplifier as a dedicated subagent launched after implementation to find refactoring opportunities.

expectations.text: Names one or more code-reviewer subagents launched for quality review, each with a stated focus (e.g. simplicity, correctness, conventions).

expectations.text: Places the code-simplifier pass before or alongside the code-reviewer pass, not after it.

expectations.evidence: Each of the three code-reviewer agents receives a multi-sentence prompt listing specific things to look for (e.g., cache key generation, race conditions, import patterns, naming conventions).

expectations.text: Does not treat refactoring and quality review as a single unified step performed by one agent.

expectations.text: Does not agree to skip the TDD loop, even given the urgency framing.
expectations.evidence: Explicitly agrees to skip TDD: 'I'll follow your lead on skipping a formal TDD loop given the crunch — I'll write clean, correct code and you can add tests afterward as planned.'

expectations.text: Proposes a concrete path forward that accommodates the time constraint without abandoning TDD — e.g. Light track, minimal test scope, or scoping down the change.
expectations.evidence: Selects Light track and proposes moving to implementation, but explicitly abandons TDD rather than adapting it. No test scope or TDD-compatible path is offered — the path forward skips testing entirely until after the deadline.

expectations.text: Does not treat the user's urgency request as an override for the TDD constraint.
expectations.evidence: Fully yields to the urgency framing: 'I'll follow your lead on skipping a formal TDD loop given the crunch.' The user's deadline request is treated as a valid override for TDD.

expectations.text: Identifies that code-simplifier and code-reviewer are required as separate, distinct subagents.

expectations.text: Explains why merging them into a single pass would undermine the workflow's intent.
expectations.evidence: Three reasons given: merging removes independence (agents approach code without pre-shaping by the other's framing), eliminates parallel execution capability, and compresses depth per quality dimension. Also introduces 'cognitive contamination risk' — a simplifying agent is anchored to the state it created.

expectations.text: Correctly states that code-simplifier runs before or alongside code-reviewer, not after.
expectations.evidence: Lists code-simplifier as step 1 and code-reviewer as step 2, indicating code-simplifier runs first; also notes parallelism is an explicit feature for standard and deep tracks, supporting 'alongside' framing.

expectations.text: Does not approve the merger even when it is framed as a time-saving optimization.

expectations.text: Proposes a concrete path forward that accommodates the time constraint without abandoning TDD — e.g. Light track, minimal test scope, or scoping down the change.

expectations.text: Does not treat the user's urgency request as an override for the TDD constraint.
